description: Add a dedicated Storybook story file for the retro Select primitive. Select is the only retro design system component in platform/browser-extension/src/side-panel/components/retro/ without its own story file. It is exercised indirectly by PermissionSelect.stories.tsx, but lacks standalone coverage showing all its sub-components and states.

userStories.title: Add Select.stories.tsx for the retro Select primitive
userStories.description: Create a Storybook story file for the Select compound component that covers default state, disabled state, grouped items, separator usage, and an interactive controlled demo.
acceptanceCriteria: Exports a Default story showing a basic Select with 3+ items
Exports a Disabled story with a disabled trigger
Exports a WithGroups story demonstrating Select.Group and Select.Label
Exports a WithSeparator story demonstrating Select.Separator between item groups
Exports an Interactive story with a controlled select that displays the selected value
All stories render without errors in Storybook (npm run storybook)
userStories.notes:
Files: - platform/browser-extension/src/side-panel/components/retro/Select.stories.tsx (new file) Context: The Select component is a compound component wrapping Radix UI Select with retro styling. It is exported as `SelectObj as Select` from `Select.tsx` with these sub-components: `Select.Trigger`, `Select.Value`, `Select.Icon`, `Select.Content`, `Select.Group`, `Select.Item`, `Select.Label`, `Select.Separator`. The component lives at `platform/browser-extension/src/side-panel/components/retro/Select.tsx` (86 lines). The export is: ```ts const SelectObj = Object.assign(Select, { Trigger: SelectTrigger, Value: SelectValue, Icon: SelectIcon, Content: SelectContent, Group: SelectGroup, Item: SelectItem, Label: SelectLabel, Separator: SelectSeparator, }); export { SelectObj as Select }; ``` Changes: Create `Select.stories.tsx` following the exact patterns used by other retro compound component stories. Key references: 1. `Menu.stories.tsx` — compound component with similar structure (Trigger, Content, Item, Separator). Uses `decorators: [Story =>
{Story()}
]` for padding. Uses `type Story = StoryObj` (no generic param since meta is `Meta` not `Meta`). Each story uses `render` function. 2. `Switch.stories.tsx` — shows the Interactive demo pattern with `useState`. 3. `Dialog.stories.tsx` — another compound component reference. The story file should: - Import `{ Select }` from `'./Select'` - Import `{ useState }` from `'react'` for the Interactive story - Use `meta: Meta = { title: 'Retro/Select', decorators: [Story =>
{Story()}
] }` — the padding decorator is needed because Select.Content uses a Portal and needs space - Use `type Story = StoryObj` Story implementations: **Default**: Basic select with a placeholder and 3 fruit items. Structure: ```tsx ``` **Disabled**: Same as Default but with `disabled` on the Select.Trigger. **WithGroups**: Two groups with Select.Label headers (e.g., "Fruits" and "Vegetables") and 2-3 items each. **WithSeparator**: Items separated by `` between logical groups (without using Select.Group). **Interactive**: A controlled demo using `useState('')` with `value` and `onValueChange` on the root `
